meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement professional user onboarding and overcome no version control with this beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To eliminate the chaos of 'no version control,' treat user onboarding as your system's state machine. By hardcoding onboarding flows into your initial build, you enforce linear progress, preventing users from triggering unversioned edge cases. Use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to maintain state integrity across your deployments.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a production environment that feels like a house of cards. A client needs a change, but there’s no Git history, no rollback, and every push feels like a gamble. You aren't just building a feature; you're playing a high-stakes game of technical debt chicken where one wrong move wipes your progress.

The secret to professional stability isn't just better commits; it's controlling the user's journey from the moment they hit your screen. By architecting a robust onboarding flow, you gatekeep the application’s state, ensuring users only interact with verified, stable features, effectively neutralizing the risks of unversioned development.

steps.title: Define the State Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid JSON schema for user progress to ensure onboarding steps are tracked consistently without database drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for UserOnboardingState with fields for current_step, completion_status, and last_updated_timestamp.

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding Engine
steps.description: Generate a state-driven component that renders onboarding steps based on the user's specific progress index.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React hook 'useOnboarding' that manages the transition between steps based on the UserOnboardingState schema.

steps.title: Implement Guard Rails
steps.description: Create logic to prevent users from accessing unversioned features until their onboarding state is marked 'complete'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks onboarding completion status before allowing access to the main dashboard routes.

steps.title: Automate State Persistence
steps.description: Ensure onboarding progress survives page refreshes, simulating the consistency of version control.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service to sync UserOnboardingState to local storage with a fallback to the database using an upsert pattern.

steps.title: Final Verification Audit
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review the entire onboarding flow for logic leaks or unhandled state transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the onboarding flow for potential race conditions or state inconsistencies.

pro_tips: Always wrap your onboarding state updates in a try-catch block to prevent UI freezes during network failures.
Use a unique 'build_id' in your local storage keys to force onboarding resets whenever you push a major update.
Implement a 'skip' flag for power users, but log this event to your analytics to track abandonment rates.
Treat your onboarding JSON as a living document; keep the schema strictly decoupled from your UI components.
Use a state machine library if your onboarding flow exceeds five distinct steps to prevent spaghetti logic.
resources: State Machine Patterns for SaaS: https://xstate.dev/docs
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, migrate your codebase to a proper Git repository immediately to transition from 'no version control' to professional CI/CD.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unversioned code dictate your freelance career. Master these AI-driven workflows to ship faster, safer, and with more authority. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
